title: mitracarpus capitatus
intro: Mitracarpus capitatus is a tropical plant species, commonly known as "Hairy Balls" due to its distinctive hairy, rounded inflorescences. The plant is used in traditional medicine, particularly in Latin America and the Caribbean, and has been found to possess antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antiparasitic properties. The medicinal properties of Mitracarpus capitatus are attributed to its ability to reduce fever, alleviate pain and inflammation, and combat various infections and parasites, thereby providing several health benefits, including relief from respiratory issues and skin conditions. The plant is typically prepared as a tea or infusion, and in some cases, it is used as a tincture or dried and used in herbal remedies.
